initialCommit.title: Create initial commit
initialCommit.reasonWorktreeMode: This mode runs each agent in its own isolated worktree, which needs a base commit to branch from.
initialCommit.explanation: This repository has no commits yet. Yoda will run `git add -A` and create an initial commit (respecting .gitignore).
initialCommit.manyFilesWarning: That's a lot of files — make sure large directories (node_modules, build output, data) are listed in .gitignore before committing.
initialCommit.failed: Failed to create the initial commit.

sidebar.hideProjectsWithoutActiveTasksDescriptio: Filters the sidebar to projects with at least one visible, non-archived task, keeping project navigation focused on current work.
sidebar.hideTasksWithoutActiveConversationsDescr: Filters the sidebar to tasks with at least one non-archived session, hiding empty tasks that have not started any session yet.
sidebar.sortNeedsReviewLastDescription: When enabled, tasks marked as unread (read later) sink to the bottom of their group, keeping actionable tasks on top.
sidebar.sortArchivingLastDescription: When enabled, a task sinks to the bottom of its group as soon as you archive it, instead of holding its place while the archive flow (pre-archive command, cleanup) runs.
sidebar.expressModeDescription: The “+” on a project row skips the home configuration and creates a task immediately: auto-named, branched off the default branch, with a session started on the last-used runtime. If the runtime or default branch is not ready yet, the home view opens instead so you can fill it in.
sidebar.copyProjectPathFailed: The project path could not be copied to the clipboard.

renameProject.title: Rename project
renameProject.label: Alias
renameProject.hint: Leave empty to use the default name ({{name}}).
renameProject.tooLong: Alias must be {{max}} characters or fewer.
renameProject.matchesName: Alias is the same as the default name; leave empty instead.

runScripts.noWorkspace: Open the task to run its scripts
runScripts.title: Run scripts — {{name}}
runScripts.description: Shell commands run at each stage of the worktree lifecycle. Saved at project level; all tasks inherit unless overridden.

mobileConnection.title: Mobile connection
mobileConnection.heading: Connect Yoda Mobile
mobileConnection.description: Scan the install code first, then scan the connection code to pair this desktop.
mobileConnection.devDescription: Open the mobile app in Expo Go for local development, then scan the connection code to pair this desktop.
mobileConnection.prodDescription: Scan the install code first, then scan the connection code to pair this desktop.
mobileConnection.devUnavailableInProd: Development connection methods are only available in a dev build of the desktop app.
mobileConnection.loadFailed: Failed to load mobile gateway details.
mobileConnection.localExpoDescription: For local iOS testing. Scan with the iPhone Camera app to open Expo Go and connect.
mobileConnection.installDescription: Scan to open the mobile download page for this product.
mobileConnection.connectDescription: Scan after installing the app. The app will open and connect to this gateway.
mobileConnection.securityNote: The connection code includes this desktop gateway token. Use it only with trusted devices on trusted networks.

settings.title: Settings

general.description: Manage your account, privacy settings, notifications, and app updates.

tasksTab.description: Configure how new tasks are created and run.

sessionsTab.description: Configure session naming, summaries, archiving, and how sessions run.

account.sessionExpiredHint: Sign in again to reconnect your Yoda account.
account.createAccountHint: Create an Yoda account to automatically connect GitHub using OAuth2.
account.signOutConfirmDescription: You will need to sign in again to reconnect your Yoda account.

defaultRuntime.title: Default runtime
defaultRuntime.description: The runtime used by default when starting a new session.

promptsTab.description: Atomic operating principles. Enabled principles are appended after the runtime's system prompt when a session starts.

prompts.description: Each principle is one atomic rule. Toggle a principle on to embed it after the runtime's system prompt; enabled principles also show in the session panel's Persona section.
prompts.textPlaceholder: Principle text appended after the system prompt

statusline.title: Statusline templates
statusline.description: Candidate statusline commands. Switch the active one from a session's Harness → Statusline section; the command receives the session JSON on stdin.

integrationsTab.title: Integrations
integrationsTab.githubCliManagedTooltip: Run `gh auth logout` in your terminal to disconnect

repositoryTab.autoUpdateGitignoreAria: Enable .gitignore updates for CLI hook configs
repositoryTab.worktreeLocationDescription: Where task worktrees are created for local projects. Each project can override this in its project settings.
repositoryTab.worktreeLocationProjectDescription: Worktrees move with the project, but running git clean -fdx in the project will delete them. Created under
repositoryTab.worktreeLocationCentralDescription: All projects share one worktree pool, one folder per project.

interfaceTab.description: Customize the appearance and behavior of the app.

terminalTab.description: Configure terminal font, history, and clipboard behavior.

keyboardShortcutsTab.description: Customize keyboard shortcuts for app actions.

openInTab.description: Choose which external apps appear in "Open in" menus and file actions across the app.

theme.ariaLightGreen: Set theme to Yoda Light Green
theme.ariaDarkGreen: Set theme to Yoda Dark Green
theme.restartForSaveReveal: Restart Yoda to use the save dialog and reveal action.
theme.overwriteDescription: "{{name}}" already exists. Importing this file will replace it.
theme.warningDescription: This theme has {{count}} readability warning(s). You can still import it.
theme.readFailed: Could not read the selected file.
theme.deleteDescription: Delete "{{name}}"? If it is active, Yoda will return to the system theme.

update.ready: Update ready. Restart {{product}} to use the new version.
update.installing: Installing update. {{product}} will close and restart automatically — this may take a few seconds.
update.errorBadge: Update temporarily unavailable — please try again later
update.unavailableInBuild: Update checks are only available in packaged builds.

telemetry.title: Privacy & Telemetry
telemetry.description: Help improve {{product}} by sending anonymous usage data.
telemetry.inactive: Inactive in this build (no PostHog keys)

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Get notified when agents need your attention.
notifications.soundDescription: Play audio cues for agent events.
notifications.osNotificationsDescription: Show system banners when agents need attention or finish (while Yoda is unfocused).

tasks.autoGenerateNameDescription: Generate a readable session title from a session's first prompt.
tasks.initTaskNameFromSession: Initialize task names from session titles
tasks.initTaskNameFromSessionDescription: When the initial session's title is generated, use it as the task name. Tasks you renamed yourself are left untouched.
tasks.branchNamingDescription: How auto-created branches are named: a time hash is final at creation; AI semantic naming renames the branch once the session title is generated.
tasks.namingConfigDescription: Agent, target language, and the context sources used to name sessions and tasks.
tasks.summaryConfigDescription: The agent used to generate session summaries. Runs on its own runtime and model, independent of each session's runtime.
tasks.namingAgentHint: The agent that names tasks. It binds both the runtime and model, since naming must run inside an agent environment.
tasks.summaryAgentHint: The agent that generates session summaries. It runs entirely on its own runtime and model, so summaries keep working even if the session's own runtime is unavailable.
tasks.namingSettingsDescription: Optional fallback model id for agent-based task naming when inference does not return a candidate.
tasks.autoTrustWorktreesDescription: Skip the folder trust prompt in Claude Code for new tasks.
tasks.autoTrustWorktreesInfo: Only applies to Claude Code. Writes trust entries to ~/.claude.json before launching.
tasks.enableTmuxDescription: Run agent sessions and terminals in tmux sessions so they survive Yoda restarts.
tasks.tmuxMissing: tmux is not installed. Sessions will run directly until tmux is available.
tasks.tmuxUnavailableDescription: This session will run directly for now. Install tmux to keep agent sessions alive across Yoda restarts.
tasks.tmuxUnavailableRemoteDescription: This remote session will run directly for now. Install tmux on the remote host to keep agent sessions alive across Yoda restarts.
tasks.tmuxInstalledDescription: tmux is ready for agent sessions and terminals.
tasks.tmuxNotDetectedAfterInstall: tmux was not detected after installation.
tasks.tmuxNoInstallCommand: No automatic tmux install command is available for this system.
tasks.preArchiveCommandDescription: When set, archiving a task or session first sends this skill or command to the target session and waits for the agent to finish. Leave empty to archive immediately.

terminal.fontDescription: Choose the font family for the terminal.
terminal.scrollbackLinesDescription: Rows kept for session scrollback and late session replay. Higher values use more memory.
terminal.autoCopySelectedDescription: Automatically copy text to clipboard when you select it in the terminal.

defaultAgent.title: Default runtime
defaultAgent.description: The runtime that will be selected by default when creating a new task.

settings.serverUnavailable: {{product}} server is currently unavailable. Please try again later.

keyboard.shortcutRemovedDescription: {{label}} no longer has a key binding.
keyboard.shortcutConflictDescription: Conflicts with "{{label}}". Choose a different shortcut.

customCommand.cliCommandTooltip: The CLI command to execute (e.g., claude, codex)
customCommand.commandPrefixTooltip: Prefix for agent-native commands and skills. Codex uses $, Claude uses /.
customCommand.resumeFlagTooltip: Flag used when resuming a session (e.g., -c -r)
customCommand.extraArgsTooltip: Extra flags appended to the command (e.g. --enable-all-github-mcp-tools)
customCommand.envVarsTooltip: Environment variables set when running the agent
customCommand.initialPromptFlagTooltip: Flag for passing initial prompt (empty means pass directly)

branchPrefix.autoPushDescription: Push the new branch to the selected project remote and set upstream after creation.
branchPrefix.autoUpdateGitignoreDescription: When Yoda writes CLI hook configs, also add their paths to .gitignore.

openInApps.tooltipNotInstalled: Install this tool to show it in menu

onboarding.signInDescription: Yoda uses your Lovstudio account for sync and personalization. You can connect GitHub separately.

import.headerSingleDescription: Found {{projectsCount}} and {{tasksCount}} from your previous Yoda installation
import.headerMultiTitle: Do you want to import projects and tasks from other Yoda versions?
import.conflictsDescription: These projects exist in both selected versions. Choose which version to keep.

greeting.earlyMorning: Early start, {{name}} — quiet hours, perfect for creating
greeting.morning: Morning, {{name}} — what shall we create today?
greeting.evening: Evening, {{name}} — what shall we make tonight?
greeting.lateNight: Up late, {{name}} — finish this one, then rest

home.headline: What shall we create today?
home.promptPlaceholder: Ask yoda anything. Type / or $ to use skills, or @ to mention files.
home.attachSshUnsupported: Local attachments are not supported for SSH projects
home.attachPasteFailedToast: Could not attach the pasted image
home.attachImagesAsPathsDesc: Reference images as @path mentions instead of pasting them natively into the agent terminal.
home.instructionFilesHint: The runtime's built-in system prompt is not editable. The global CLAUDE.md acts as your user-level system prompt; the project's CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md are the project-level prompts. Click to expand, hover a row to open and edit.
home.voiceTooltip: Trigger the Typeless hands-free voice shortcut
home.voiceTriggerFailedToast: Could not trigger Typeless voice input
home.modeBrainstormDesc: Clarify requirements into PRD, UI/UX notes, design, tasks, and acceptance checks.
home.modeCompareDesc: Start several agents on isolated branches.
home.modeReviewDesc: Implementation and reviewer agents loop on the same branch.
home.modeTeamDesc: CEO agent decomposes work for specialist agents.
home.reviewRoundLimit: Review loop stops after {{count}} rounds if issues remain.
home.taskConversationScopeTooltip: You are creating a child session for this task.
home.subtaskScopeTooltip: You are creating a subtask of this task.
home.needProjectHint: Pick a project to send a message.
home.strategyAria: Choose how to start the task
home.strategyNoWorktreeDesc: Fastest path. Changes land on the current branch in place — no isolation. Best for tiny fixes or solo work.
home.strategyNewBranchDesc: Spins up an isolated copy on a new branch. Keeps {{branch}} clean and lets multiple tasks run in parallel.
home.strategyCheckoutExistingDesc: Checks out this existing branch in an isolated worktree — no new branch, and your project directory stays untouched.
home.reviewStrategySameBranchDesc: A and B share the current branch. Best when the loop should directly refine the working tree.
home.reviewStrategyNewBranchDesc: A and B share one isolated review branch while keeping {{branch}} clean.

conversations.emptyDescription: Create a conversation to start working with an agent.
conversations.deleteDescription: "{{title}}" will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.

stats.linesTitle: Lines changed by this task (committed + working tree)

sessionInfo.title: Session info
sessionInfo.noSessionDescription: Create or open a session to inspect runtime details.
sessionInfo.summaryManualPlaceholder: Write a summary for this session.
sessionInfo.summaryManualHint: Saving a non-empty summary pins it until you clear it or regenerate.
sessionInfo.overviewSummaryPlaceholder: No summary yet — click to write one, or generate with AI.

fieldInfo.contentSource: On-disk source file for this session's content (Claude JSONL transcript / Codex rollout). Transcript, stats, and naming all read from it.
fieldInfo.autoApprove: Whether agent tool calls are approved automatically, without per-call confirmation.
fieldInfo.resumeCommand: CLI command you can run in an external terminal to resume this session manually.
fieldInfo.agentStatus: Agent working state inferred from session output: idle, working, awaiting input, etc.
fieldInfo.runtimeRunning: Whether the runtime CLI process is currently running.
fieldInfo.tmux: Whether the session is hosted in tmux. If enabled, the process survives window close and can be reattached.
fieldInfo.ptyStatus: Connection state between the UI and the main-process pseudo terminal (PTY); affects live terminal output.
fieldInfo.ptySessionId: Internal identifier of this PTY connection, for debugging only; changes after app restart.
fieldInfo.processStatus: Momentary state inferred from process activity: busy, idle, or waiting.
fieldInfo.processUpdatedAt: When the process state (busy/idle/waiting) last changed.
fieldInfo.sessionExited: Whether the CLI process has exited. Continuing the conversation spawns a new process via the resume command.
fieldInfo.seen: Whether you have viewed this session's latest output; drives the unread marker in the sidebar.
fieldInfo.updatedAt: When this session's database record was last written. Any field change (title, status, archive, ...) refreshes it.
fieldInfo.lastInteractedAt: When you last sent input or interacted with the terminal in this session — unlike record updated, which background writes also refresh.

hooks.title: Hooks
hooks.unsupportedDescription: This runtime does not expose configurable hooks.
hooks.noneDescription: No hooks were found in the active settings for this session.
hooks.debugDescription: Log every executed hook. Restarts the session to take effect.

rename.title: Rename task
rename.configureHint: These settings apply to all tasks. Adjust, then regenerate.
rename.label: Task name
rename.currentContextHint: This is a preview of the context that will be used if you regenerate now, not the original naming record.
rename.noContextNoRecord: This task has no saved record of the context used for automatic naming. The panel will show current sources when available; regenerating will collect and save context with the current settings.
rename.noContextUnavailable: There is no naming context to show right now. The task may be missing an initial prompt, the project may not be mounted, or all context sources may be disabled.
rename.noContextEmptySources: Yoda tried to collect naming context, but the enabled sources were empty. Check the context settings and regenerate to read the prompt, project, README, and recent task titles again.
rename.duplicate: A task with this name already exists in this project.
rename.invalidSlug: Task name must contain at least one letter, digit, or CJK character.

archiveWithNote.title: Archive "{{name}}"
archiveWithNote.label: Note (optional)
archiveWithNote.placeholder: Why are you archiving this task?
archiveWithNote.skillDescription: Sent to every live session before archiving; waits for the agent to finish. Edit or extend the preset, or leave empty to archive directly.

addRemote.title: Add Remote
addRemote.rejectedHint: Remote already has commits. Linking succeeded, but integrating histories must be resolved manually.

finish.title: Finish task
finish.errorNotMergeable: This task has no mergeable branch.
finish.errorBaseNotCheckedOut: Check out {{branch}} in the project root first (currently on {{current}}).
finish.errorBaseDirty: The project root checkout on {{branch}} has uncommitted changes.

create.initialPromptPlaceholder: Describe what you want the agent to do.

terminals.title: Terminals
terminals.emptyDescription: Create a terminal to run commands in this task workspace.
terminals.scriptsEmptyDescription: Configure setup / run / teardown scripts in project settings; their output shows up here.

panel.contextUnsupported: Context inspection is not available for this runtime yet.
panel.renameContextHint: Context sent to the naming model
panel.noTranscript: No transcript yet — start the conversation to populate.
panel.systemPromptHint: Claude Code does not log the base system prompt; only CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md instructions and runtime context are visible below.
panel.codexSystemPromptHint: Codex records base instructions and developer messages in the rollout transcript.
panel.memoriesHint: Long-term memories the agent maintains on its own (~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/), distinct from human-authored instructions like CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md.
panel.noMemories: No memories yet — the agent accumulates these as it works
panel.memoriesUnsupported: Codex has no self-maintained memory store
panel.statuslineHint: The status bar command at the bottom of the CLI. Switching a template writes back into the settings file that defines it (defaults to ~/.claude/settings.json).
panel.noStatuslineTemplates: No templates yet — add them in Settings → Agents
panel.copyFilePathFailed: The file path could not be copied to the clipboard.

taskInfo.readInstructionValue: Read the JSONL file above; parse one JSON object per line and extract user/assistant messages in chronological order.

context.copyFailed: The value could not be copied to the clipboard.

sessionPanel.summaryEmptyDescription: A summary appears once there is something to summarize.

sessionPanel.summaryFailedDescription: Summary generation failed. It will retry after the next reply.

browser.title: Browser
browser.emptyHint: Enter a URL, or click a link in the terminal

sidePane.cardDescSession: Session info, prompts, and the agent runtime
sidePane.cardDescChanges: Review file changes in the worktree

setParent.crossProject: Parent task must be in the same project

transcript.earlierLines: {{count}} earlier lines — open the full file

mcp.httpUnsupported: Some agents don't support HTTP servers and are disabled.

promptLibrary.title: Prompts
promptLibrary.empty: No saved prompts yet. Create one to reuse it when composing a task.

form.title: Title
form.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional short note about when to use this prompt

automation.title: Automation
automation.subtitle: Save recurring prompts as automations and run them in the internal workspace with one click.

form.title: Name
form.cronHint: Standard cron (min hour day month weekday). Example: 0 9 * * * runs every day at 09:00.
form.promptPlaceholder: Describe what the agent should do when this automation runs.

delete.title: Delete automation?
delete.description: "{{name}}" will be deleted. This action cannot be undone.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.subtitle: Manage installed plugins and the skills, commands, and agents they bundle

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Extend your agents with reusable skill modules
skills.catalogDescription: Skills from the <openai>OpenAI</openai> and <anthropic>Anthropic</anthropic> catalogs. Install a skill to make it available across all your coding agents. Skills follow the open <standard>Agent Skills</standard> standard. If you want to use skills from another library, feel free to let us know through the feedback modal.

validation.name: Name must be lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens (2-64 chars).

create.instructionsPlaceholder: Write the skill instructions here. The YAML frontmatter (name and description) will be added automatically.
create.instructionsHint: Define what this skill does and how agents should use it

detail.usageTrendEmpty: No calls in the last 30 days

triggerTest.title: Trigger test
triggerTest.hint: Check whether this skill gets invoked when it should. Each query starts one headless Claude session and uses a small amount of quota; runs are manual only.
triggerTest.empty: Generate queries with AI or add them manually to verify the skill's trigger behavior.
triggerTest.queryPlaceholder: Type what a user might say…
triggerTest.reviseInstruction: Improve the description field in this skill's frontmatter so the failing trigger tests below pass. Change only the description field; keep the body and other fields untouched. Failing tests:
triggerTest.failExpectedTrigger: expected to trigger, but did not
triggerTest.failExpectedNoTrigger: expected not to trigger, but it did

revise.title: AI revise {{skill}}
revise.hint: Describe the change you want (description or body). The AI proposes a revision, shows a diff, and only writes SKILL.md after you confirm.
revise.instructionPlaceholder: e.g. Make the description state the trigger scenarios precisely; merge the body steps into 3…

fork.title: Duplicate {{skill}}
fork.instructionPlaceholder: Leave empty for a plain copy. With an instruction the AI rewrites the copy, e.g. adapt it for Python projects…
fork.reviseFailed: Copy created, but the AI transform failed — edit it manually

agentManager.title: Agents
agentManager.subtitle: Build reusable agents from a system prompt, skills and a preferred runtime.
agentManager.preferredRuntimeHint: Used when available; otherwise falls back to the mode default.
agentManager.systemPromptPlaceholder: Describe this agent's role, constraints and how it works.
agentManager.noSkills: No installed skills. Install skills first to enable them here.

general.name: General Agent
general.description: A general-purpose coding agent with no special framing.

spec.name: Spec Agent
spec.description: Spec-first: clarify requirements and produce a PRD before coding.

review-implementer.name: Implementer
review-implementer.description: Implements the requirement and addresses reviewer feedback.

review-reviewer.name: Reviewer
review-reviewer.description: Reviews an implementation against the requirement.

team-ceo.name: CEO
team-ceo.description: Decomposes the requirement and assigns work to the team.

team-product.name: Product
team-product.description: Turns the requirement into product behavior and acceptance criteria.

team-engineering.name: Engineering
team-engineering.description: Implements code changes with engineering rigor and validation.

team-uiux.name: UI/UX
team-uiux.description: Shapes the user experience and implements UI changes.

team-operations.name: Operations
team-operations.description: Reviews rollout, onboarding, communication, and operational risk.

naming.name: Naming
naming.description: Generates concise task names and branch slugs.

summary.name: Summary
summary.description: Summarizes a coding session from its messages.

agents.title: Runtime
agents.subtitle: Manage installed CLI runtimes — detection status and versions

maas.title: Runtime metadata
maas.description: Static CLI metadata Yoda uses when launching this agent.
maas.placeholder: Runtime capabilities and launch details for {{name}}.
maas.cliFlagsDescription: Flags Yoda uses to launch this agent

account.title: Auth source
account.description: Pick the auth source {{name}} sessions use. Other sources can stay configured and ready to switch.
account.officialSubscriptionDescription: {{name}} keeps official login and subscription credentials in its own CLI or local config.
account.officialApiDescription: API keys and base URLs forwarded to {{name}} when Yoda launches runtime sessions.
account.maasDescription: Use configured model platforms for shared routing, observability, and billing when available.
account.selectedUnavailable: This source is not ready yet — finish configuring it first
account.accountNotLoggedIn: No signed-in account detected. Sign in via the {{name}} CLI first.
account.accountUnsupported: Reading {{name}} subscription account info is not supported yet.
account.noKnownApiEnvVars: No known API environment variables are registered for this runtime.
account.noMaasConnections: No MaaS platform is connected yet.
account.usageNoMaas: Connect ZenMux in the MaaS view to show real usage statistics.

memory.title: Memory / context file
memory.description: Global memory file used by {{name}} (e.g. CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / GEMINI.md).
memory.unsupported: {{name}} does not expose a memory file location.

hooks.title: Hooks
hooks.noPath: No hook configuration path is known.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: Directory {{name}} reads skills from. Manage centrally in the Skills view.

models.title: Models
models.description: Sync available models for this agent and choose which ones to show. The list is refreshed from a shared public model directory and cached locally.
models.emptyDescription: Sync models to populate the choices available for this agent.

settings.execDescription: Override the launch command, CLI, and extra args for {{name}}.
settings.namingDescription: Optionally override the model {{name}} uses for automatic task titles and branch slugs. Leave it empty to use the model you already run this agent with.
settings.namingModelPlaceholder: Leave empty to use the current model
settings.namingCommandHint: Yoda replaces {model} with the configured or inferred model; prompt is sent on stdin unless the template contains {prompt}.
settings.configDescription: Open the agent's local config directory or file in Finder.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.subtitle: Deeply researching how each agent designs and implements these details is slow, hard work — but it is the only way to make Yoda feel seamless. Every capability below is backed by hands-on research, kept correct and up to date.

book.bookSubtitle: based on source-level analysis of mainstream agents and best practices
book.desc: We run this research like writing a book: each capability gets a deep dive into how different agents design and implement it, captured as a standalone, continuously-iterated report. Together, the reports build toward an authoritative guide. The matrix below is the book's table of contents and writing progress.

matrix.title: Runtime capability matrix
matrix.desc: Per-runtime support status for each capability. Click a row for the research notes behind it.

version.name: Version management
version.desc: Detect the installed CLI version, surface updates, and pin known-good versions.

install.name: Installation
install.desc: One-click install of the runtime CLI with the official install channel.

launch.name: Launch
launch.desc: Spawn and orchestrate runtime sessions from Yoda with the right flags and environment.

doctor.name: Config self-diagnosis
doctor.desc: Health-check runtime configuration: detect misconfiguration and suggest fixes.

sessionResume.name: Resume / fork / history
sessionResume.desc: Resume, continue and fork past sessions — seamless cross-process takeover.

compaction.name: Context compaction
compaction.desc: Context-window management and auto-compaction so long sessions don't lose memory.

checkpointing.name: Checkpoint / rewind
checkpointing.desc: File-level checkpoints and rollback — an undo button when the agent breaks code.

memory.name: Auto memory
memory.desc: Manage the agent's self-accumulated long-term memory, guarding against pollution and staleness.

fileContext.name: Files & multimodal input
fileContext.desc: @file references, images and ignore rules — every channel that feeds context.

plugins.name: Plugins
plugins.desc: Detect, validate and manage plugins and marketplace distribution.

subagents.name: Subagents
subagents.desc: Detect, validate, debug and manage subagent definitions.

slashCommands.name: Custom commands
slashCommands.desc: Detect, validate, debug and manage slash / custom commands.

permissions.name: Permissions & approvals
permissions.desc: A unified view and management of permission modes / approval policies.

sandboxing.name: Sandboxing
sandboxing.desc: Detect and manage OS-level sandbox configuration (Seatbelt / landlock etc.).

managedSettings.name: Managed settings
managedSettings.desc: Detect and reconcile organization-level policy rollout.

trustModel.name: Trust model
trustModel.desc: Unified management of folder trust, hook trust and MCP trust.

modelConfig.name: Model selection & config
modelConfig.desc: Unified management of model tiers, reasoning effort and related settings.

providers.name: Third-party providers / gateway
providers.desc: Configuration management for BYOK, API-base redirection and LLM gateways.

worktrees.name: Git worktree parallelism
worktrees.desc: Create, merge and clean up worktree-isolated parallel tasks.

codeReview.name: Code review
codeReview.desc: Integrate and productize agent code-review capabilities.

headlessCi.name: Headless / CI
headlessCi.desc: Non-interactive mode and CI pipeline integration.

sdk.name: Agent SDK
sdk.desc: Research the SDK integration path (versus the current CLI-spawn approach).

ideAcp.name: IDE / ACP
ideAcp.desc: Editor integration and the Agent Client Protocol.

notifications.name: Notifications
notifications.desc: Unified takeover of done / needs-attention notification channels.

keybindings.name: Keybindings & themes
keybindings.desc: Detect and manage keybinding and theme configuration.

planMode.name: Plan mode
planMode.desc: Plan-mode state awareness and approval-flow integration.

agentTeams.name: Multi-agent orchestration
agentTeams.desc: Agent teams and parallel task orchestration.

routines.name: Routines / scheduled tasks
routines.desc: Unattended tasks via cron scheduling and event triggers.

remoteExecution.name: Cloud execution & handoff
remoteExecution.desc: Cloud sessions and cross-device handoff (teleport / remote).

transcript.name: Transcript storage format
transcript.desc: Parsing JSONL / rollout session files — the foundation under every sync capability.

sessionNameSync.name: Session name sync
sessionNameSync.desc: Keep session names consistent between the CLI and Yoda, no matter where they are renamed.

sessionAutoRename.name: Session auto-rename
sessionAutoRename.desc: Generate meaningful session titles automatically when the runtime can't — or won't — do it well.

sessionStateSync.name: Session state sync
sessionStateSync.desc: Reflect the real running/idle/finished state of every session in real time.

authSync.name: Auth sync & management
authSync.desc: Detect subscription / API-key auth per runtime and manage credentials in one place.

usageSync.name: Usage sync & management
usageSync.desc: Aggregate per-account usage and cost across runtimes.

mcp.desc: Detect, validate, debug and manage MCP server configuration.

hooks.name: Hooks

statusline.name: Statusline
statusline.desc: Detect, validate, debug and manage the statusline configuration.

sessionNameSync.claude: Names change through several channels (in-CLI rename, transcript replay, rename inside Yoda) — Yoda merges all sources without overwriting each other.
sessionNameSync.codex: Same multi-source merge as Claude Code; Codex adds its own rollout-file naming path that must be reconciled.

sessionAutoRename.claude: Claude Code's built-in auto-naming is English-only; Yoda generates a localized title and overrides it.
sessionAutoRename.codex: Auto-rename only exists in the Codex app, not the CLI — Yoda fills the gap by generating titles itself.

sessionStateSync.claude: Early versions inferred state from JSONL transcripts, which left zombie "working" sessions; switching to PID-based detection made it solid.
sessionStateSync.codex: Porting the PID-based approach — currently in testing.

usageSync.claude: Reads the real plan tier from the keychain and computes cost with ccusage-compatible accounting — no fabricated numbers.

skills.codex: Codex is stricter about skill format; Yoda's built-in validator surfaces issues right in the sidebar.

hooks.codex: Codex hooks went GA on 2026-05-14 (10 events + content-hash trust model), closely mirroring CC's event set — Yoda integration under research.

statusline.codex: Codex has a built-in statusline (tui.status_line in config.toml plus /statusline), though custom command-backed rendering isn't supported yet — Yoda integration under research.

cta.title: Shape the roadmap

usage.title: Usage
usage.subtitle: Lifetime activity across all tasks and sessions — token burn parsed from agent transcripts.
usage.empty: No usage data yet — run some agent sessions first.
usage.loadingHint: First load parses all session history — this can take a moment.

caliber.tasks: How it's counted: tasks with a non-null archive date (archiving is the completion act). The sub-line is all tasks ever created.
caliber.tokens: How it's counted: parsed from local Claude / Codex session transcripts, including subagent transcripts and any auxiliary directories configured in project settings. Total = input + output + cache read + cache write. Other runtimes have no transcript reader and count as zero.
caliber.lines: How it's counted: per task = committed diff vs source branch (merge-base) + uncommitted working-tree changes. Live for active tasks; archived tasks use their last snapshot (tasks archived before this feature shipped have none and count as zero).
caliber.activeDays: How it's counted: local calendar days with any token usage in session transcripts.
caliber.heatmap: How it's counted: each session's tokens are bucketed into the local date they were consumed. Color levels are quantiles (25/50/75) of non-zero days.
caliber.byProject: How it's counted: each session is attributed to its task's project; untracked auxiliary-directory sessions go to the project that configured the path (sessions tracked by another project stay there — the global total never double-counts).
caliber.byModel: How it's counted: each transcript row is attributed to its provider-reported model id (Claude: message.model; Codex: the active turn_context model). Rows without one count as unknown.
caliber.byRuntime: How it's counted: grouped by the runtime that ran each session; only sessions with parseable transcripts are included.
caliber.bySource: How it's counted: the account mode recorded when each session was spawned. Sessions from before tracking landed are attributed to the runtime's current setting (estimate).
caliber.topTasks: How it's counted: sum of all session tokens per task, top 10.

aiLogs.title: AI Logs
aiLogs.subtitle: Every AI invocation the app makes — background CLI runs, API calls, and interactive sessions — with timings and full details for debugging.
aiLogs.emptyDescription: Background AI work (naming, summaries, logo generation, …) will be logged here as it runs.

One absolute path per line; paths that no longer exist still work, transcripts are matched by the recorded working directory.
